title: digitalis purpurea subsp. dubia
intro: Digitalis purpurea subsp. dubia, also known as Digitalis dubia, is a subspecies of the common foxglove plant, a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. This plant contains cardiac glycosides, particularly digoxin and digitoxin, which have been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various heart conditions, including atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The medicinal properties of Digitalis dubia have been found to improve heart function, reduce symptoms of heart failure, and lower blood pressure, making it a valuable herb for cardiovascular health. Digitalis dubia is often prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and capsules, and should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity and side effects.
